_meta.doc: Single source-of-truth summary for kanji-scope enforcement across the N5 corpus. Created 2026-05-14 per ISSUE-007. The per-surface rules below are ALREADY enforced by three CI invariants (JA-13, JA-28, JA-40) — this file documents which surface gets which rule + acts as the canonical reference. The actual allowed-kanji data lives in (a) data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json (the strict 106-kanji base list) and (b) data/dokkai_kanji_exception.json (per-surface additions: 'dokkai-passage' and 'paper-distractor'). When adding a new surface or relaxing a rule, update this file FIRST so the reasoning is recorded, then update the invariants accordingly.
consumers_planned: tools/check_content_integrity.py (JA-13 / JA-28 / JA-40 — to be refactored to read this file rather than the underlying sources directly, when safe to do so)
Future linters / authoring helpers that need 'what kanji may surface X use'
consumers_current: JA-28 reads data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json + dokkai-passage entries from data/dokkai_kanji_exception.json
JA-40 reads data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json + paper-distractor entries from data/dokkai_kanji_exception.json
_meta.drift_risk_mitigated_by: All three invariants read the SAME underlying whitelist (n5_kanji_whitelist.json) — the only file that contains the canonical strict-N5 character set. The exception file (dokkai_kanji_exception.json schema v2) carries per-surface additions tagged with a 'surfaces' array, ensuring the same exception cannot drift between dokkai vs paper-distractor scope. The 2026-05-08 schema v2 migration explicitly addressed ISSUE-007 + IMP-005 by making the exception entries surface-tagged.

grammar.json.description: Grammar pattern catalog. Patterns, examples, common_mistakes, summary.
grammar.json.skip_subtree_rationale: These fields intentionally show cross-level register variation (humble/respectful keigo, native-grade error illustration) and would over-flag if N5-strict-enforced. Documented exemption since round-7 2026-05-06.

reading.json.description: Old reading-practice tab (separate from the dokkai mock-paper flow).
reading.json.note: Reading-practice tab STAYS strict even though the paper-flow dokkai (papers/dokkai/*.json) is relaxed. Different pedagogical surfaces.

dokkai-passage.rationale: Authentic JLPT N5 mock-paper passages legitimately include kanji slightly above the strict whitelist (e.g. 京, 付) because those passages mirror what JEES actually publishes.

paper-distractor.description: Moji / goi / bunpou paper question DISTRACTORS (wrong-answer choices). Correct answer must stay strict N5.
paper-distractor.rule: N5 + per-surface exceptions FOR DISTRACTORS ONLY
paper-distractor.constraint: JA-40 explicitly checks that any non-whitelist kanji appears ONLY in distractor positions, never as correct answer. Exception kanji as correct answer = JA-40 fail.

kanji.json (study cards).description: Kanji-study surface — by definition shows each whitelist kanji as the primary entity.
kanji.json (study cards).rule: STRICT N5 (the 106 kanji ARE the content)
allowed_kanji_source: data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json — same set as the whitelist itself
kanji.json (study cards).enforced_by: (no specific invariant; the count match is verified by data/n5_kanji_whitelist.meta.json#expected_count == count(kanji.json#entries))
